other_observers: Bruce Wild; Jane Gabrilove first found the bird on December 17 in her backyard 2 blocks from where I saw it. Then on December 25 it was seen in a different yard one block away. Then later on December 26 Todd Deininger saw the bird just a few houses away from whe
specific_location: I saw it on Cornell Drive a few houses west of Stanford Lane. This is about a mile or two south of McIntosh Reservoir.
description: Gray back with orange wing stripes. It has a orange belly and throat with a black breast band. It had white undertail coverts. The head is mostly black with a orange stripe above the eye.Behaviors: It was mostly on the ground feeding on anything it could find with a large group of American Robins. The size of the bird was just slightly smaller than the nearby robins.Call: It was giving a chip note similar to that of a Hermit Thrush, it was a chup note, but a little harder than a HETH.Plumage: Adult
